differences.title: 1. State Management
differences.leftDesc: Emphasizes data that cannot be changed after creation, reducing bugs.
differences.rightDesc: Objects hold mutable state, which can be modified over time.

differences.title: 2. Core Concept
differences.leftDesc: Functions produce same output for same input, no side effects.
differences.rightDesc: Encapsulates data and behavior into reusable objects.

differences.title: 3. Control Flow
differences.leftDesc: Focuses on what to achieve, not how to achieve it.

differences.title: 4. Side Effects
differences.leftDesc: Aims to isolate and manage side effects carefully.
differences.rightDesc: Side effects are a natural part of state changes.

differences.title: 5. Reusability
differences.leftDesc: Functions can take other functions as arguments or return them.
differences.rightDesc: Code reuse through class hierarchies or combining objects.

differences.title: 6. Concurrency
differences.leftDesc: Immutability simplifies concurrent programming and prevents race conditions.
differences.rightDesc: Managing shared mutable state in concurrent environments is challenging.

differences.title: 7. Testability
differences.leftDesc: Pure functions are deterministic and easy to unit test.
differences.rightDesc: Testing objects often requires mocking dependencies and state.
